issues.title: Toast notifications lack ARIA live region and keyboard dismiss for all severities
issues.description: The ToastEntry component renders toasts without an aria-live='polite' or role='alert' wrapper. Screen readers never announce toast messages. The Escape key dismiss only works when focus is inside the toast container, but toasts never receive focus automatically. Error toasts that persist indefinitely are invisible to assistive tech users.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry function component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The outermost container div has no ARIA attributes and the toast is not announced to screen readers. The Escape key listener requires focus inside the container but focus is never moved there. AFTER: 1) Add role='status' and aria-live='polite' to the toast container div for success/info/warning toasts. For error severity, use role='alert' and aria-live='assertive'. 2) Add aria-atomic='true' to ensure the full message is read. 3) Remove the document-level keydown listener that checks containerRef focus. Instead, add a global Escape key handler at the ToastProvider level that dismisses the most recent toast regardless of focus location. 4) Ensure the dismiss button has aria-label='Dismiss notification'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Breadcrumbs truncation and touch targets fail on 375px viewport
issues.description: The FolderBreadcrumbs component uses a horizontal flex layout with no overflow handling. On 375px screens with 3+ folder levels, breadcrumbs overflow the viewport causing horizontal scroll. The crumbButtonStyle sets minHeight and minWidth to 44px but the chevron separators between crumbs are not wrapped in tappable targets, reducing effective touch area below Apple HIG 44x44px minimum.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The breadcrumb container renders all crumbs inline with no overflow strategy. On narrow screens, the full path overflows horizontally. ChevronRight separators are bare icons without touch padding. AFTER: 1) Wrap the breadcrumb container in a div with overflow='hidden', whiteSpace='nowrap', and textOverflow='ellipsis'. 2) On mobile (use the existing isMobile state), collapse intermediate breadcrumbs into an ellipsis button '...' that shows only the root and the current folder. Tapping '...' reveals a dropdown with the full path. 3) Wrap each ChevronRight separator inside a span with minWidth='24px' and display='flex' alignItems='center' justifyContent='center' to prevent it from being a dead zone between tap targets. 4) Add aria-label='Navigate to [folder name]' on each breadcrumb button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Toast auto-dismiss timer does not pause on hover or keyboard focus
issues.description: The ToastEntry has timer pause/resume logic via remainingRef and startedAtRef but the actual mouseenter/mouseleave and focus/blur event handlers that would call clearTimeout and restart the timer are not wired to the container element. Users reading a long toast message or interacting with the action button may have the toast disappear mid-interaction.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry function component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component initializes remainingRef and startedAtRef for timer tracking but does not attach onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave or onFocus/onBlur handlers to the container div. The auto-dismiss timer runs regardless of user interaction. AFTER: 1) On the outermost container div of each toast, add onMouseEnter and onFocus handlers that clear timerRef.current via clearTimeout and store the elapsed time in remainingRef (remainingRef.current = remainingRef.current - (Date.now() - startedAtRef.current)). 2) Add onMouseLeave and onBlur handlers that restart the timer with the remaining duration: startedAtRef.current = Date.now() and timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => handleClose(toast.id), remainingRef.current). 3) Only apply this behavior when TOAST_DURATION[toast.severity] is not null. 4) Add a visible countdown progress bar at the bottom of each auto-dismissing toast: a thin 3px bar using the severity border color that shrinks from 100% to 0% width over the toast duration using a CSS transition. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No global skip-to-content link or focus management on route change
issues.description: The App component has no skip navigation link for keyboard and screen reader users. When navigating between routes via the sidebar, focus remains on the clicked sidebar link instead of moving to the main content area. This violates WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) and makes keyboard navigation tedious as users must tab through the entire sidebar on every page change.
issues.prompt: In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The App renders sidebar and main content area without a skip navigation link. Route changes do not manage focus. AFTER: 1) Add a visually hidden skip link as the first child inside the App's outermost container: an anchor element with text 'Skip to main content' that links to '#main-content'. Style it with position='absolute', top='-40px', left='0', and on focus, set top='0' with a z-index above the sidebar, background of colors.primary, color white, padding 8px 16px, and borderRadius. 2) Add id='main-content' and tabIndex={-1} to the main content wrapper div (the element that contains the page routes). 3) Add a useEffect that listens to location changes (from useLocation) and on each change calls document.getElementById('main-content')?.focus() with a small setTimeout of 100ms to ensure the new page has rendered. 4) Add role='main' to the main content wrapper. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No centralized skeleton loader component matching design system specs
issues.description: The Global Standards acceptance criteria require skeleton loaders that match final layout geometry with pulse animation on every data-dependent page. Currently there is no reusable Skeleton primitive in Primitives.tsx. Pages either show nothing during loading, a generic spinner, or inconsistent ad-hoc skeletons. This violates the design standard of 'skeleton screens, not spinners' and causes layout shift when data arrives.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Primitives.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The Primitives file exports Card, MetricBox, Btn, Tag, and other shared components but has no Skeleton, SkeletonRow, SkeletonCard, or SkeletonMetric component. AFTER: 1) Add a base Skeleton component that accepts width (string, default '100%'), height (string, default '16px'), borderRadius (string, default borderRadius.md from theme), and style override. It renders a div with background using a linear-gradient animation: background='linear-gradient(90deg, #E5E7EB 25%, #F3F4F6 50%, #E5E7EB 75%)' with backgroundSize='200% 100%' and a CSS keyframe animation that translates the background from left to right over 1.5s infinite. Use an inline style with animation property. 2) Add SkeletonRow: renders a horizontal flex container with a 40px circle skeleton (avatar), a column of two text skeletons (200px and 120px wide), and a right-aligned 80px skeleton. Height 48px matching table row spec. 3) Add SkeletonCard: renders a card-shaped skeleton with the standard Card padding, containing a 24px icon skeleton, a 100px label skeleton, and a 160px value skeleton, matching MetricBox layout. 4) Add SkeletonTable that accepts rowCount (default 5) and renders a header row of 4 skeletons plus rowCount SkeletonRow components. 5) Export all four components. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's design system uses a mature component library (Blueprint internally) with comprehensive skeleton states, WCAG AA compliance baked in, and a dedicated accessibility team. Fieldwire's mobile-first approach includes haptic feedback on touch interactions and iOS-native gesture support. SiteSync's design system primitives exist but lack the skeleton infrastructure, accessibility foundations (skip nav, ARIA live regions, focus management), and mobile collapse patterns that enterprise GCs expect. The toast system is partially built but missing critical UX patterns (hover pause, screen reader announcement) that competing products handle correctly.
summary: The UI Design System module scores 41/100 with its strongest areas in visual polish (62) and TypeScript quality (58) but critical gaps in accessibility (35), mobile responsiveness (40), and data richness (35). The five identified issues target the highest-impact gaps: toast notifications are invisible to screen readers and dismiss during user interaction, breadcrumbs break on mobile viewports, route changes abandon keyboard focus, and there is no reusable skeleton loader system despite design standards requiring them on every page. These are foundational design system deficiencies that cascade to every page in the application. Fixing the skeleton primitives alone would improve loading states across all 16+ pages, while the accessibility fixes (skip nav, ARIA live, focus management) are WCAG compliance requirements that enterprise GCs increasingly demand in procurement checklists.
